criteria.criterion: Guide mode can take the user from a saved Paper.design YouTube tutorial to a visible Ask AI-style shader card using deterministic substeps and user correction.

customizationQuestion: What should the Ask AI-style Paper card say, and should Spanks prioritize speed or visual polish?
extractedSummary: The video shows how to recreate a viral Ask AI-style social graphic in Paper.design. This demo version breaks each step into concrete substeps with a Good enough checkpoint: create a 520x180 dark rounded card, add a 116x116 left circle, place Liquid Metal inside it, add white Ask AI text, add a sparkle icon, apply Neuron Noise with Multiply to the text, add a rounded Pulsing Border, and stop at visual verification.

gotchas.description: The tutorial notes that elements below in Paper's layers panel may appear above visually, which can surprise Figma users.
gotchas.fix: If the metal shader, circle, or card disappears, inspect layer order before changing the design.
gotchas.title: Paper layer order feels reversed

gotchas.description: The video uses Remix Icon and SVG copy/download, but a live demo should not get stuck browsing icon libraries.
gotchas.fix: Use any available sparkle/star icon if SVG import is slow, then continue to the shader effects.
gotchas.title: Icon sourcing can derail the demo

gotchas.description: The creator experiments with colors, blend modes, scale, brightness, and border colors rather than following exact numeric values.
gotchas.fix: Make one visible improvement, verify readability, and move on instead of over-tuning.
gotchas.title: Shader tuning is intentionally fuzzy

prerequisites.detail: The video operates inside the Paper editor. If the browser shows marketing, signup, login, or an empty workspace instead of an editable canvas, pause and ask the user to sign in or open a canvas.
prerequisites.title: Paper editor access

prerequisites.detail: The video pulls a sparkle icon from Remix Icon. For the demo, any visible sparkle/star SVG or icon is acceptable if Remix Icon slows things down.
prerequisites.title: Have a sparkle icon fallback

prerequisites.detail: Browser Use can be flaky inside Paper's editor. For the deterministic demo, use Spanks as a screen-observing guide after the setup checkpoint.
prerequisites.title: Use Guide me for the live demo

promptSnippets: After setup, prefer Guide me: observe the current Paper canvas, point at likely controls, and let the human click.
If Paper asks for login, signup, payment, or project selection, ask the user to complete that step manually.
Show the user the substeps and Good enough checkpoint before moving to the next step.
Use the deterministic demo values: card 520x180 radius 44 fill #151515, circle 116x116 fill #2F7DFF, Ask AI white 44px, sparkle/star 40px.
Prefer visible labels and concepts from the video: rectangle, border radius, circle, shaders, Liquid Metal, sparkle SVG, Ask AI, Neuron Noise, Multiply, Pulsing Border.
Make visible design progress quickly: one metal shader, one text shader, one border effect.
Do not chase export during the live demo. End when the Ask AI shader card is visibly complete.

skill.name: paper-shaders-design-guide
skill.title: Create an Ask AI shader card in Paper
skill.yamlContent:
name: paper-shaders-design-guide
steps:
  - title: Open an editable Paper canvas
  - title: Handle login or canvas handoff
  - title: Draw the dark rounded card
  - title: Add the left circle
  - title: Add Liquid Metal to the circle
  - title: Add the Ask AI text
  - title: Add a sparkle icon
  - title: Add Neuron Noise to the text
  - title: Add the Pulsing Border
  - title: Verify the final graphic

steps.description: Open Paper and get to an editable canvas before drawing anything.
steps.gotchaText: If the browser lands on login, signup, pricing, or a static gallery, pause and ask the user to open an editable Paper canvas.
steps.title: Open an editable Paper canvas
steps.value:
1. Open https://paper.design/
2. Sign in if Paper asks
3. Create or open any editable canvas
Good enough: a blank editable Paper canvas is visible
steps.verification: A Paper canvas is visible and ready for shapes. Marketing pages, signup pages, or static galleries are not good enough.

steps.description: Use this as the human checkpoint for account, file, or project setup.
steps.gotchaText: Do not attempt to create accounts, enter private credentials, or change billing settings.
steps.title: Handle login or canvas handoff
steps.value:
1. Complete login manually if needed
2. Pick any safe demo file or blank canvas
3. Press I'm done - continue
Good enough: the canvas is editable and Spanks can continue from this screen
steps.verification: The user has confirmed the editable Paper canvas is ready.

steps.description: Create the base shape for the Ask AI social graphic.
steps.gotchaText: Paper's layers feel reversed compared with Figma: lower items in the layers panel can appear visually above. If the circle disappears, check layer order.
steps.target: Rectangle tool, Fill, Radius, Width, Height
steps.title: Draw the dark rounded card
steps.value:
1. Select Rectangle
2. Draw a wide horizontal card
3. Set W 520 / H 180 if fields are visible
4. Set radius 44 if visible
5. Set fill #151515
Good enough: a dark rounded card is on the canvas
steps.verification: A single dark rounded rectangle is visible. Exact dimensions are optional if it visually reads as a wide card.

steps.description: Add the circle that will receive the metal shader.
steps.gotchaText: If the circle disappears, check layer order before redrawing it.
steps.target: Ellipse tool, Fill, Width, Height, Align
steps.title: Add the left circle
steps.value:
1. Select Ellipse or Circle
2. Draw a circle inside the left side of the card
3. Set W 116 / H 116 if fields are visible
4. Set fill #2F7DFF
5. Center it vertically inside the card
Good enough: a blue circle sits inside the left side of the card
steps.verification: The blue circle is visible on top of the card and roughly centered vertically.

steps.description: Apply one obvious metal shader treatment to the circle.
steps.gotchaText: If Liquid Metal is not visible by exact name, choose the most obvious metal-looking shader and keep moving.
steps.target: Shaders panel, Liquid Metal shader, opacity/background control
steps.title: Add Liquid Metal to the circle
steps.value:
1. Open Shaders or Effects
2. Choose Liquid Metal
3. Place it over the blue circle
4. Set background opacity to 0 if visible
5. Resize it to fit inside the circle
Good enough: the circle looks metallic
steps.verification: The left circle area now has a metallic shader texture while staying inside the card.

steps.description: Add the main label from the tutorial graphic.
steps.gotchaText: If exact font controls are hard to find, prioritize readable white text over exact typography.
steps.title: Add the Ask AI text
steps.value:
1. Select Text
2. Type Ask AI
3. Set color #FFFFFF
4. Set size 44 if visible
5. Place it to the right of the circle
Good enough: white Ask AI text is readable inside the card
steps.verification: The card clearly reads Ask AI in white text.

steps.description: Add the small visual cue next to the Ask AI text.
steps.gotchaText: If importing SVG is slow, skip the icon import and use any visible sparkle/star icon or simple placeholder.
steps.target: Icon, star, sparkle, or simple placeholder
steps.title: Add a sparkle icon
steps.value:
1. Add any sparkle/star icon
2. Set color #FFFFFF
3. Set size about 40
4. Place it left of Ask AI
5. Skip if icon sourcing is slow
Good enough: a sparkle, star, or simple placeholder is visible
steps.verification: A sparkle/star icon or acceptable placeholder appears between the circle and text.

steps.description: Apply one visible texture treatment to the text without over-tuning.
steps.gotchaText: Do not over-tune. Apply one visible Neuron Noise treatment and advance.
steps.target: Neuron Noise shader, blending mode, color controls
steps.title: Add Neuron Noise to the text
steps.value:
1. Add Neuron Noise
2. Place it over the Ask AI text only
3. Set blend to Multiply if visible
4. Set scale about 2 if visible
5. Set brightness about 70% if visible
Good enough: the text has subtle texture but stays readable
steps.verification: The Ask AI text has a subtle blue-gray shader/noise treatment while remaining readable.

steps.description: Frame the card with the final shader effect from the tutorial.
steps.gotchaText: If color controls are not visible, keep the default border and advance. The key visible payoff is the border framing the card.
steps.target: Pulsing Border shader, roundness, opacity, color controls
steps.title: Add the Pulsing Border
steps.value:
1. Add Pulsing Border
2. Place it around the card
3. Set roundness to 100% if visible
4. Set background opacity to 0 if visible
5. Use blue/green colors if controls are visible
Good enough: a glowing rounded border frames the card
steps.verification: A pulsing or glowing rounded border frames the card without covering the icon or Ask AI text.

steps.description: Compare the result to the tutorial target and stop at visible completion.
steps.gotchaText: Do not chase export in the live demo. The win is that the saved YouTube tutorial became an active guided session.
steps.title: Verify the final graphic
steps.value:
Check: dark rounded card
Check: metal circle
Check: Ask AI text
Check: sparkle icon or placeholder
Check: text texture
Check: pulsing border
Good enough: a visible Ask AI shader card exists
steps.verification: The user can point to the finished Ask AI-style graphic on the Paper canvas.
